response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack and backend software engineer with a solid track record in web, mobile, and low-latency systems, but his profile is fundamentally misaligned with the Principal Performance Architect role. The position demands deep expertise in SoC/IP modeling, GPU architecture, hardware-software co-design, and deep-learning workload optimization at the silicon level — none of which appear anywhere in his resume. His most hardware-adjacent work (C/C++ at National Instruments, low-latency DLang microservices at Sociomantic) is entirely software-layer performance work with no crossover into AI accelerator architecture, SIMD/MIMD design, or chip-level performance analysis.
strengths: Over 15 years of diverse engineering experience including low-latency, high-throughput systems (millions of HTTP req/s at Sociomantic) demonstrating real performance engineering instincts
Hands-on C and C++ background at National Instruments writing components for an engineering IDE, indicating some exposure to systems-level development
Recent AI-adjacent work at re:cinq involving MCP code generators and agentic workflow infrastructure, showing awareness of the AI tooling space
concerns: Zero experience with SoC/IP modeling, GPU architecture, or any hardware design or simulation — the core technical requirements of this role
No background in deep neural network training or inference optimization, SIMD/MIMD architectures, or AI accelerator workload analysis
Title and trajectory are Staff/Senior Software Developer (web/backend focus), not IC architect; no evidence of system architecture design at the hardware or chip level
